content.problemStatement:
Given an array of size NNN and two integers KKK and SSS, the special sum of a subarray is defined as follows:

(Sum of all elements of the subarray) * (KKK - ppp * SSS)

Where ppp = number of distinct prime factors of “product of all elements of the subarray”.

Find the maximum special sum by considering all non-empty subarrays of the given array.

###Input


First line contains 3 integers NNN, KKK and SSS.
Second line contains NNN integers, the elements of the array.
###Output

Output a single integer. The maximum special sum considering all non-empty subarrays of the array.

###Constraints:


1≤N,K,S≤1051 \leq N, K, S \leq 10^51≤N,K,S≤105
0≤K/S≤200 \leq K / S \leq 200≤K/S≤20
1<1 \lt1< Any element of array <105\lt 10^5<105
###Sample Input

4 10 2

14 2 7 15

###Sample Output

138

###Sample Explanation

Consider the subarray {14, 2, 7}

Total number of distinct prime factors in it is 2 (2 and 7).

Therefore, value of special sum is (14 + 2 + 7) * (10 - 2 * 2) = 138.

This is the subarray with the maximum special sum.
